-{"id":"bd-sb8p2","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] apply_fcw_commit silently falls back to bytes.clone() on merge-bytes None, risking lost updates if preflight/apply diverge","description":"# Review finding (review-only pass on 2026-04-23)

## Location

`crates/ffs-mvcc/src/lib.rs` lines ~2095-2102 inside `apply_fcw_commit`, and the mirrored block ~2182-2189 inside `commit_ssi_internal`.

## Finding

When `self.latest_commit_seq(block) > snapshot.high`, the apply path re-runs the merge:

```rust
proof.merge_bytes(&base, &latest, &bytes)
    .unwrap_or_else(|| {
        tracing::error!("preflight_fcw missed an unmergeable conflict on block {block:?}");
        bytes.clone()
    })
```

If `merge_bytes` returns `None`, the fallback silently keeps our staged `bytes`, overwriting the concurrent writer's version. That is a classic lost-update; the only signal is a `tracing::error!` line.

## Why this is currently unreachable (and why it still matters)

`preflight_fcw` calls `preflight_resolve_block_conflict`, which routes through `resolved_write_bytes` and returns `CommitError::Conflict` on a None merge. Because `commit_fcw_internal` runs preflight then apply under the same `&mut self`, no other writer can mutate `versions` in between, so the inputs to `merge_bytes` are identical, so the outputs match. The fallback is defensive code for an invariant that currently holds.

The risk is future refactors: if anyone (a) separates preflight and apply without holding the MvccStore write lock across them (the `commit_fcw_prechecked` contract is 'should hold', not enforced), or (b) introduces GC/compaction between preflight and apply, or (c) changes how `version_bytes_at` resolves, the fallback silently corrupts data rather than aborting the commit.

## Suggested fix (small)

Replace `unwrap_or_else` with `debug_assert!(false, ...)` followed by the fallback, OR hoist the merge resolution earlier (before the `let Transaction { .. } = txn` destructure) so the commit can abort with `CommitError::Conflict` on merge failure — matching the `resolved_writes_for_commit` pre-journal flow already used by `commit_transaction_journaled` in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:4347`.

## Acceptance

- apply_fcw_commit and commit_ssi_internal abort (or panic in debug) on merge_bytes None rather than silently overwriting.
- Regression test: inject a transaction whose merge proof refuses at apply time (e.g., MergeProof::Unsafe with a real conflict passed into the internal apply stage via test hook) and assert the commit returns `CommitError::Conflict`.
- Existing commit/merge tests still pass.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] — latent data-loss risk. Not currently reachable under the documented locking contract, but the fallback is the kind of silent defensive branch that hides invariant violations rather than surfacing them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:09.432555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:09.432555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Consider using test isolation or thread-local subscriber.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:34:00.322261613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:32:04Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:32:04Z","close_reason":"Serialized the move-ext log-contract tests behind a shared guard and factored subscriber installation so tracing::dispatcher::set_default plus rebuild_interest_cache no longer race across concurrent tests; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-fuse dispatch_ioctl_move_ext_ -- --nocapture and rch cargo clippy -p ffs-fuse --lib -- -D warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sex56","title":"add mounted-path btrfs hard-link unlink nlink-accounting coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:18.624382643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:21:19.404245506Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T04:21:19.402144429Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. See commit message bodies of prior fuse_e2e.rs work for per-bead test citations (bd-wu5y3: fuse_hard_link_to_symlink_preserves_symlink_type + btrfs_fuse_*; bd-ynh1e: fuse_rename_over_nonempty_directory_reports_enotempty; bd-p8uaj: btrfs_fuse_rename_over_same_inode_hardlink_is_noop; bd-sex56: btrfs_fuse_hard_link_unlink_updates_nlink_accounting; bd-7xfny: fuse_xattr_ext4_value_too_large_reports_einval + boundary tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sf6w","title":"Bitmap CRC: verify kernel bitmap pre-processing before checksumming","description":"Read the Linux kernel source for ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set() and ext4_block_bitmap_csum_verify() to determine:
(a) Whether the kernel zeroes unused tail bits in the bitmap before checksumming. For a group with fewer blocks than blocks_per_group, the tail bits may be set to 1 (indicating 'used') by mkfs but the kernel may ignore/zero them before CRC.
(b) Whether the kernel checksums exactly block_size bytes or clusters_per_group/8 bytes. Our code checksums the full block.
(c) Whether the kernel XORs or masks any fields in the group descriptor before storing the checksum.

SPECIFIC FILES TO CHECK:
- fs/ext4/balloc.c: ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set(), ext4_block_bitmap_csum_verify()
- fs/ext4/bitmap.c: ext4_bitmap_csum()
- include/linux/ext4.h: struct ext4_group_desc layout

ACCEPTANCE: Document the exact kernel pre-processing steps. If any differ from our implementation, fix our code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:25.650024112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:28.787877259Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:28.787389015Z","close_reason":"Kernel source confirmed: no group number in bitmap CRC, length is clusters_per_group/8 for blocks and inodes_per_group/8 for inodes. Fixed in commit 20d65f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crc32c","ext4","investigation"]}
